articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]

images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Oyarzabal hat trick leads Spain to friendly win over Andorra
article.description: Attacking midfielder Mikel Oyarzabal climbed off the bench to score three times in the second half and help Spain to a 5-0 friendly victory over Andorra on Wednesday in their penultimate warm-up game before Euro 2024.
article.linkText: Oyarzabal nets hat trick in 5-goal Spain rout

article.story:
Attacking midfielder Mikel Oyarzabal climbed off the bench to score three times in the second half and help Spain to a 5-0 friendly victory over Andorra on Wednesday in their penultimate warmup game before Euro 2024.

Spain manager Luis de la Fuente fielded a much changed squad which, despite dominating from the start, struggled to turn 75% of possession into scoring opportunities in a dour first half against their lowly rivals who came in to the match on a 15-game winless streak.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Former Newcastle United and Leicester City forward Ayoze Pérez, who was making his international debut at the age of 30, gave the hosts the lead in the 24th minute and was Spain's lone bright spot in a dour first half.

He was a constant menace running up and down the left channel and made his case for a place in the final 26-man squad which coach De la Fuente will name on Saturday.

Real Sociedad's Oyarzabal, who was one of four substitutions at half-time, extended Spain's lead in the 53rd minute with a tidy finish from a low cross by Ayoze, who almost scored what would have been Spain's third with a brilliant angled volley that was denied on the goalline by a defender.

"It was a dream debut," Ayoze, who scored five goals in Betis' last seven LaLiga games this season, told TVE. "To score a goal and give an assist it's just great, I'm delighted. Every ball I controlled, every run I made, I put it all together towards my goal that is making the team.

"I tried to seize the opportunity in every practice session and in the game. Now it's up to the coach to make the best decision for the team. It is a special opportunity. Negative thinking will only distract you from your goal."

It was Oyarzabal who ended up scoring Spain's third from a quick counter in the 66th and he got his hat trick in the 73rd when he fired home a rebound from inside the box.

Ferran Torres wrapped a comprehensive victory eight minutes later on another counter-attack.

Coach De la Fuente will cut three players from his squad before Spain face Northern Island in Mallorca on Saturday in their final warm-up game before they start their Euros campaign on June 15 against Croatia in Group B. They will also take on Italy and Albania.

"It will be hard to decide on the three discards. Those who have come are already part of the group. I have arguments to keep all of them..." De la Fuente told a press conference.

"Ayoze and others showing this level of play does not surprise me. Given the level, I think this team can do something important in the Euros. The decision will be very well thought out."

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Spain 1, Andorra 0. Ayoze Pérez (Spain) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Pau Cubarsí with a headed pass   following a corner.
First Half ends, Spain 1, Andorra 0.
Second Half begins Spain 1, Andorra 0.
Substitution, Spain. Jesús Navas replaces Marcos Llorente.
Substitution, Spain. Marc Cucurella replaces Álex Grimaldo.
Substitution, Spain. Mikel Oyarzabal replaces Álvaro Morata.
Substitution, Spain. Robin Le Normand replaces Pau Cubarsí.
Substitution, Andorra. Éric Vales replaces Marc Valés because of an injury.
Goal!  Spain 2, Andorra 0. Mikel Oyarzabal (Spain) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Ayoze Pérez.
Substitution, Spain. Fermín López replaces Pedri.
Substitution, Andorra. Josep Gómes replaces Iker Álvarez.
Goal!  Spain 3, Andorra 0. Mikel Oyarzabal (Spain) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Ferran Torres   following a fast break.
Substitution, Spain. Martín Zubimendi replaces Aleix García.
Goal!  Spain 4, Andorra 0. Mikel Oyarzabal (Spain) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal.
Substitution, Andorra. Dacu replaces Marc García.
Substitution, Andorra. Ludovic Clemente replaces Chus Rubio.
Goal!  Spain 5, Andorra 0. Ferran Torres (Spain) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Fermín López with a through ball.
Substitution, Andorra. Márcio Vieira replaces Joel Guillén.
Substitution, Andorra. Marc Pujol replaces Cucu.
